prs.title: feat(codegen): Phase 3 — Cranelift JIT codegen crate
prs.body:
## Summary

Implements the complete `codegen` crate: CoreExpr → Cranelift IR compilation with GC-safe stack maps, effect machine driver, and GC frame walker.

### Wave 1: Scaffold (#29)
- `CodegenPipeline` — double-compile strategy (extract stack maps, then define for execution)
- `VMContext` — `#[repr(C, align(16))]` with alloc_ptr, alloc_limit, gc_trigger
- `StackMapRegistry` — BTreeMap-based lookup by return address
- `emit_alloc_fast_path` — bump-pointer allocation as Cranelift IR with GC slow path
- `host_fns` — gc_trigger, heap_alloc, heap_force (extern "C" for JIT)

### Wave 2A: Emitter (#31, #32, #33, #37, #38)
- `emit/expr.rs` — CoreExpr → Cranelift IR (Lit, Var, App, Lam, LetNonRec, LetRec, Con, PrimOp)
- `emit/case.rs` — Case dispatch via br_table (DataAlt) and comparison chains (LitAlt)
- `emit/join.rs` — Join points as parameterized Cranelift blocks
- `emit/primop.rs` — PrimOp → Cranelift instructions (Int/Word/Double/Char arithmetic + comparisons)
- `gc/frame_walker.rs` — x86_64 RBP chain walker for GC root discovery
- GC audit: stack map correctness verified for all safepoints

### Wave 2B: Effect Machine (#30)
- `Yield` enum — Done/Request/Error result type
- `CompiledEffectMachine` — step/resume protocol driving JIT-compiled freer-simple stacks
- `ConTags` struct for type-safe constructor tag passing

### Quality Improvements (#34, #35, #36)
- **Structured errors**: `ValueKind` enum, `ArityMismatch`/`TypeMismatch` split in EvalError, `MissingConstructor`/`FieldCountMismatch`/`UnexpectedValue` in EffectError
- **Display/From impls**: VarId, JoinId, DataConId, Literal, PrimOpKind, AltCon + From impls for Literal
- **Layout type enums**: `HeapTag`, `ThunkStateTag`, `LitTag` (`#[repr(u8)]`) with payload offset constants
- **TreeBuilder** moved from core-testing to core-repr as public API

## Test plan

- [x] 301 tests pass (`cargo test --workspace`)
- [x] Scaffold gate tests (JIT boot, VMContext offsets, stack maps, alloc fast path)
- [x] Emitter tests (expr, case, join, primop — 30+ integration tests)
- [x] Effect machine tests (Yield::Done, Yield::Request, error paths)
- [x] GC frame walker tests (root finding, rewriting, JIT boundary)
- [x] GC audit tests (stack map presence at all safepoints)
- [x] Quality PR tests (Display roundtrips, HeapTag/LitTag roundtrips, error variants)

prs.title: feat(core-optimize): complete optimization pass pipeline
prs.body:
## Summary

Implements the full core-optimize crate with 6 optimization passes and a fixed-point pipeline:

- **Pipeline** (`pipeline.rs`): Fixed-point pass iteration with `run_pipeline`, `optimize`, and `default_passes` convenience API. MAX_PIPELINE_ITERATIONS=1000 safety bound.
- **Occurrence Analysis** (`occ.rs`): Per-variable Dead/Once/Many counting across all binding forms.
- **Beta Reduction** (`beta.rs`): Eliminates manifest `App(Lam)` redexes via capture-avoiding substitution.
- **Case Reduction** (`case_reduce.rs`): Reduces `Case` of known constructor/literal, substituting case binder and field binders.
- **Inlining** (`inline.rs`): Eliminates single-use `LetNonRec` bindings. Never inlines `LetRec`.
- **Dead Code Elimination** (`dce.rs`): Drops dead `LetNonRec` bindings and fully-dead `LetRec` groups (atomic).
- **Partial Evaluation** (`partial.rs`): First-order constant propagation — folds known PrimOps, reduces known Case scrutinees, eliminates known `LetNonRec` bindings.

Default pipeline order: BetaReduce → Inline → CaseReduce → Dce → PartialEval.

Universal property verified: `eval(pass(e)) == eval(e)`.

## Test Coverage

- 48 unit tests across all modules
- 6 proptest-based integration tests (200 cases each) verifying eval-preservation for each pass individually, the composed pipeline, and pipeline idempotency

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test --package core-optimize` — 54 tests pass
- [x] `cargo clippy --package core-optimize -- -D warnings` — zero warnings
- [x] `cargo test --workspace` — all workspace tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

prs.title: feat: expand PrimOp coverage + desugar eqString/take (fixes #64)
prs.body:
## Summary

Fixes #64: `==` on String and `take` fail with `UnboundVar` at runtime.

**Root cause:** GHC at `-O2` rewrites `== @[Char]` → `eqString` and `take` → `$wunsafeTake` via RULES and worker-wrapper transformation. Neither function has an unfolding in `.hi` files, so `Tidepool.Resolve` can't inline them → `UnboundVar` at eval time.

**Fix (two merged sub-PRs):**

1. **Hardcoded desugarings** (PR #68, already on main): Added `eqString` and `$wunsafeTake` desugarings in `Translate.hs`, plus fixed `Char` boxing in `unpackCString#`/`unpackAppendCString#` desugarings. Added 3 new test cases (`prelude_take_cons`, `prelude_eq_string_true`, `prelude_eq_string_false`).

2. **Expanded PrimOp coverage** (PR #69, this branch): Added ~41 new primop variants across Tiers 2-3 — Int bitwise, Word arithmetic+bitwise, Float ops, narrowing, type conversions, and `IndexOffAddrOp_Char`. This prevents future `Unsupported primop` crashes when GHC fuses string operations.

## Test plan
- [x] `prelude_eq_string_true` passes (string equality)
- [x] `prelude_eq_string_false` passes (string inequality)  
- [x] `prelude_take_cons` passes (take on cons-chain list)
- [x] All existing suite tests continue to pass
- [x] `cargo check --workspace` passes

prs.title: feat: Add Ask effect with two-tool continuation pattern
prs.body:
## Summary

Adds an `Ask` effect that lets Haskell programs suspend execution mid-evaluation to ask the calling LLM a question, then resume when the LLM responds.

### How it works

**Two-tool pattern** (eval + resume):

1. Haskell program calls `send (Ask "Which one?")` → execution suspends
2. `eval` tool returns `{"suspended": true, "continuation_id": "cont_1", "prompt": "Which one?"}`
3. LLM calls `resume` tool with `{"continuation_id": "cont_1", "response": "the second one"}`
4. Execution continues with the response as the Ask result

### Architecture: Channel-based suspension

Instead of parking JIT continuations (complex pointer/ownership management), the eval thread **blocks on a channel** when Ask is hit:

- `AskDispatcher` wraps existing effect handlers, intercepts the Ask tag
- On Ask: sends `Suspended` message via tokio channel, blocks on `std::sync::mpsc::recv()`
- MCP server receives suspension asynchronously, returns JSON to LLM
- On resume: sends response via channel, unblocks eval thread
- Thread continues execution (may hit more Ask effects or complete)

### Changes

All changes in `tidepool-mcp` only — zero modifications to the effect system, codegen, or runtime.

- `ask_decl()` — declares `data Ask a where Ask :: Text -> Ask Text`
- `AskDispatcher` — wraps handler stack, intercepts Ask tag via `DispatchEffect`
- `EvalSession` + `SessionMessage` — channel-based communication between eval thread and MCP server
- `ResumeRequest` — new tool parameter type
- Modified `eval()` — channel-based instead of thread-join-based
- New `resume()` method — sends response, awaits next message
- TTL cleanup — 5 minute timeout for parked continuations
- 5 new unit tests

### Usage

```haskell
-- In an eval call:
name <- send (Ask "What is your name?")
send (Print ("Hello, " <> name))
age <- send (Ask ("How old are you, " <> name <> "?"))
pure (name <> " is " <> age <> " years old")
```

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo check -p tidepool-mcp` — no errors
- [x] `cargo test -p tidepool-mcp` — 12 tests pass (7 existing + 5 new)
- [x] `cargo check --workspace` — no breakage in other crates
- [ ] Manual E2E test with MCP server (requires tidepool-extract on PATH)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

- **EffectMachine driver** (`effect_machine.rs`): Compiled state machine for freer-simple effect dispatch

prs.title: main.core optimize.case reduce
prs.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR implements the `CaseReduce` optimization pass in `core-optimize/src/case_reduce.rs`.

### Key Features
- **case-of-known-con**: Reduces case expressions where the scrutinee is a `Con` node.
- **case-of-known-lit**: Reduces case expressions where the scrutinee is a `Lit` node.
- **DataAlt matching**: Correctly matches `DataAlt` by tag and binds all fields.
- **Case binder substitution**: Substitutes the case binder with the scrutinee value in the body.
- **Default fallback**: Correctly uses the default alternative when no specific alternative matches.
- **Capture-avoiding substitution**: Utilizes `core_repr::subst::subst`.

### Implementation Details
- **Arity check**: Added a check to ensure `DataAlt` binder count matches constructor field count before reduction, for safety (addressing Copilot feedback).
- **Single reduction**: The pass performs one reduction per `run()` invocation as per the spec's requirement to "find the first reducible Case and rebuild the tree."
- **Local helpers**: Tree manipulation helpers (`extract_subtree`, `replace_subtree`, etc.) are implemented locally as explicitly instructed by the spec to avoid external dependencies for this leaf task.
- **Performance**: While Copilot suggested avoiding `Vec` allocation in `get_children`, I have adhered to the spec's provided implementation sketch for this pass.

### Verification
- **Unit tests**: All 7 test cases pass, including evaluation preservation checks.
- **Clippy/Check**: The code is clippy-clean and passes type checking.

- Implemented top-down tree traversal for identifying dead bindings.
- `LetNonRec` bindings are dropped if the binder is `Occ::Dead`.

prs.title: feat: preserve Haskell error message strings
prs.body:
This PR threads the error message from GHC Core through to the JIT runtime, so that `error "msg"` returns `YieldError::UserErrorMsg("msg")` instead of a generic message.

Changes:
- **`haskell/src/Tidepool/Translate.hs`**: Intercepts `error` and `patError` calls, recursively unwrapping `PushCallStack` to find the `LitString` message. It then wraps the `0x45` error sentinel in an `App` with the `LitString`.
- **`tidepool-codegen/src/emit/expr.rs`**: Added `extract_error_message` and `emit_error_poison` to statically detect string literals in `LetRec`/`LetNonRec` error branches and pre-allocate 3-capture lazy poison closures. Also fixed an existing indentation bug in the simple bindings phase.
- **`tidepool-codegen/src/host_fns.rs`**: Implemented `runtime_error_with_msg` and `error_poison_ptr_lazy_msg`. Modified the existing 1-capture `poison_trampoline_lazy` to inspect its argument: if it's a `LitString` (as generated by the new `App` wrapper), it dynamically extracts the string and logs/preserves it.
- **`tidepool-codegen/src/yield_type.rs`**: Added `YieldError::UserErrorMsg(String)` to correctly propagate the error string up to the `run` caller.
- **`tidepool-codegen/src/emit/case.rs`**: **Fixed a pre-existing cascading `SIGILL` bug.** `emit_case_trap` now accepts the `merge_block` and performs a `jump` with the poison pointer (if an error is in-flight) instead of unconditionally issuing a `builder.ins().trap(...)` which was causing a hard process crash when testing unresolved variables.
- **`tidepool-runtime/tests/test_error_msg.rs`**: Added integration tests to verify that standard `error "..."` and missing-pattern `patError`s correctly propagate their strings through the entire pipeline.

This PR fixes the `gc_audit` test failures by ensuring that stack map declarations for heap pointers are correctly propagated across block boundaries and into nested contexts.

### Changes:
- **`tidepool-codegen/src/emit/mod.rs`**: Added `declare_env` to re-declare environment heap pointers at block boundaries.
- **`tidepool-codegen/src/emit/case.rs`**: Integrated `declare_env` into case alternatives and merge blocks.
- **`tidepool-codegen/src/emit/join.rs`**: Integrated `declare_env` into join RHS and merge blocks.
- **`tidepool-codegen/src/emit/expr.rs`**: Integrated `declare_env` into `App` emission and added missing stack map declarations for runtime error results.

prs.title: Fix Group A SIGILL and memory faults via recursive unboxing and accurate translation
prs.body:
This PR addresses the SIGILL and memory faults found in Group A tests: `memchr`, `isPrefixOf`, `splitOn`, and `filter_array`.

## Changes Made:
- **Recursive Unboxing**: Implemented recursive loops in JIT unboxers (`unbox_int`, `unbox_addr`, `unbox_bytearray`, `unbox_float`, `unbox_double`) to properly handle deeply nested "box" constructors created by GHC (e.g., `Text (ByteArray (ByteArray# ...))`).
- **Translation Updates**: Reverted aggressive `stripBoxCon` from `Translate.hs` but kept it restricted to single-constructor product types (as done in Group C) within DataCon wrappers. This resolves "CASE TRAP" errors.
- **Robust Heap Layout Extraction**: Addressed the SIGSEGV related to missing offset tracking for string bounds and nested constructors.
- **Pointer Validation**: Embedded safe guards in byte array and text FFI helpers within `host_fns.rs` to detect invalid pointers instead of silently faulting.
- **SplitOn Assertion Update**: Handled the initial crashing bug, leaving `splitOn` failing gently on an assertion for subsequent investigation regarding nested serialization. 

All targets have been built cleanly. Tested locally with 4/5 Group A tests passing successfully.

prs.title: feat: implement heap_force and force closures in case dispatch
prs.body:
This PR implements thunk forcing in the JIT to resolve the "Closure in case scrutinee" issue identified in Group B tests.

### Changes
- **Implemented `heap_force`** in `host_fns.rs`: This host function now handles 0-capture `Closure` objects by calling their `code_ptr` with the `(vmctx, self, arg)` calling convention (where `self` is the closure and `arg` is null). This matches the JIT's emission for lambdas and thunks.
- **Updated `emit_data_dispatch`** in `case.rs`: Added a runtime check before data constructor dispatch. If the scrutinee is a `Closure` (tag 0) or `Thunk` (tag 1), it calls `heap_force` and uses the result for the matching logic.
- **Added `test_group_b_simple_thunk`**: A new regression test in `sort_crash.rs` that verifies thunks inside cons-lists are correctly forced before being case-matched.

### Verification
- `test_group_b_simple_thunk` passes.
- Existing `sort_crash.rs` tests (non-ignored) all pass.
- The original Group B tests (e.g., `test_aeson_lens_nth`) still encounter `CASE TRAP` on a `Closure`, but debug tracing revealed that these closures are actually the **poison closure** (due to earlier `UserError` calls in the `lens` package during translation/codegen). The forcing logic itself is active and correct, as proven by the new simple test.

### Note on calling convention
The `heap_force` function uses the 3-argument signature `fn(vmctx, self, arg)` for closures, which was found to be the consistent convention for JIT-compiled closures in this codebase. Passing `self` ensures that even if the closure was used for something else, it has its context. Passing `null` for `arg` is appropriate for forcing a thunk.

prs.title: feat: normalize constructor tags and fix Maybe unboxing
prs.body:
This PR fixes the Group C issue where `Map` operations (and potentially other higher-order functions) failed with `SIGILL` or `CASE TRAP` due to constructor tag mismatches.

### Changes
1.  **Stable VarIds**: Implemented `stableVarId` in `Translate.hs` using GHC's `Fingerprint` on the fully-qualified name (Module + OccName). This ensures that the same logical constructor has the same tag regardless of whether it's compiled directly or loaded via a fat interface.
2.  **Module Normalization**: Added robust module name normalization to handle GHC 9.12's `GHC.Internal.*` module structure, ensuring that internal and public aliases of the same module hash to the same ID.
3.  **Worker/Wrapper Unification**: Updated `varId` to use the worker's stable ID for both DataCon workers and wrappers. This prevents tag mismatches when a constructor is applied via its wrapper but cased on using its worker tag.
4.  **Box Stripping Fix**: Fixed a bug in `stripBoxCon` that was incorrectly unboxing sum types like `Maybe`. By restricting stripping to single-constructor algebraic types (product types), we ensure that `Just` and other sum constructors retain their pointer arguments as expected by their workers.
5.  **Test Enablement**: Enabled `test_map_difference_with` and `test_map_intersection_with` in `tidepool-runtime`, which now pass.

### Verification
-   `cargo test -p tidepool-runtime --test sort_crash` passes with 220 tests.
-   Confirmed that `Maybe` and `Int` constructors now have consistent tags across different compilation contexts.
-   Verified that `test_map_difference_with` and `test_map_intersection_with` no longer trap.

prs.title: feat: fix LetRec topological sort and closure capture
prs.body:
Fixes an infinite loop / segfault bug in recursive text functions like
`T.split`. The root cause was that top-level module bindings are compiled
as a single large LetRec group. During compilation, closures were
allocated in Phase 1 but their capture slots remained uninitialized until
Phase 3a'. If the evaluation of a deferred simple binding in Phase 3c
triggered an allocation (e.g. `newByteArray#`) and exhausted the JIT
nursery, the ensuing garbage collection root tracing would scan the
uninitialized closure capture slots and segfault.

This PR resolves the issue by zero-initializing closure capture
slots upon allocation and implementing a full transitive dependency
topological sort for LetRec deferred bindings. The capture slots are
now incrementally filled as their dependencies resolve, ensuring safe
execution and GC tracing.

Additionally:
- Corrects `Clz8#` primop implementation to narrow to I8 before clz.
- Adds comprehensive text splitting tests to `repro_split.rs`.

**Updates:**
- Addressed Copilot review feedback: removed unused imports, removed global env set `TIDEPOOL_DUMP_TREE` from test, optimized LetRec topo sort using DFS reachability instead of full transitive map building, refactored Phase 3c updates into O(1) map lookups, zero-initialized Con fields in Phase 1, and restored `#[ignore]` on `test_kv_keys_text_split_on` specifying the newly-identified bridge text mismatch issue.

prs.title: feat(gc): panic on forwarding table lookup failure during compaction
prs.body:
This PR escalates a GC forwarding failure from `eprintln!` to `panic!` in `tidepool-heap/src/gc/compact.rs`.

A missed forwarding-table lookup during GC compaction indicates a fundamental violation of GC invariants—a reachable object was not traced. Continuing with a stale `ThunkId` would result in a dangling reference, leading to silent and hard-to-diagnose corruption. Making this fail loudly with a `panic!` ensures that such bugs are caught immediately.

Verified with `cargo check -p tidepool-heap` and `cargo test -p tidepool-heap`.

prs.title: fix: update current pointer after GC in heap_force
prs.body:
In `tidepool-codegen/src/host_fns.rs`, the `heap_force` function holds a `current` pointer to a thunk. At line 334 (in the original file), it calls the thunk's entry function, which may trigger a GC. After the call, `current` might point to the from-space copy of the thunk. Subsequent writes to set the indirection and state update would then go to from-space, leaving the to-space copy in a permanent `BLACKHOLE` state.

This PR adds a forwarding check after the JIT call in `heap_force`. If `current` was forwarded during the call, it is updated to point to the to-space copy by following the forwarding pointer at offset 8.

Verified by running `cargo check` and `cargo test` in `tidepool-codegen`. All tests pass.

prs.title: feat(haskell): implement GHC 9.12 harness with CBOR serialization
prs.body:
This PR implements the Haskell harness for Tidepool, providing a pipeline to translate GHC Core (GHC 9.12) into a flat node array and serialize it to CBOR.

### Key Features:
- **GHC 9.12 Pipeline**: Parses, typechecks, and desugars Haskell source files into simplified Core.
- **Translation Layer**: Translates `CoreExpr` to a flat array of nodes with type erasure and constructor/primop saturation.
- **CBOR Serialization**: Matches the exact wire format expected by the Rust `read_cbor` implementation.
- **Metadata Extraction**: Collects and serializes DataCon metadata.

### Deliverables:
- `haskell/src/Tidepool/GhcPipeline.hs`
- `haskell/src/Tidepool/Translate.hs`
- `haskell/src/Tidepool/CborEncode.hs`
- `haskell/app/Main.hs`
- `haskell/tidepool-harness.cabal`
- `haskell/cabal.project`
- `haskell/test/Identity.hs` (Test input)

### Verification:
- Built successfully with `cabal build`.
- Successfully processed `test/Identity.hs` and `test/Types.hs` (temp file used for verification), producing valid `.cbor` files.
- Verified `meta.cbor` contains expected DataCon metadata.

prs.title: feat: implement TAG_THUNK handling in heap_force and heap_to_value
prs.body:
This PR implements `TAG_THUNK` handling in `heap_force` and `heap_to_value_inner`, as specified in the lazy thunk implementation plan.

Key changes:
- `tidepool-codegen/src/host_fns.rs`: Updated `heap_force` to handle the three thunk states:
    - `Unevaluated`: Transitions to `BlackHole`, executes the thunk code, writes the indirection, and updates the state to `Evaluated`.
    - `BlackHole`: Triggers a `RuntimeError::BlackHole` (infinite loop detection).
    - `Evaluated`: Returns the cached indirection pointer.
- `tidepool-codegen/src/host_fns.rs`: Added `RuntimeError::BlackHole` and `runtime_blackhole_trap`.
- `tidepool-codegen/src/heap_bridge.rs`: Updated `heap_to_value_inner` to recursively follow indirections for `Evaluated` thunks or return an error for other states.
- `tidepool-codegen/src/yield_type.rs`: Added `YieldError::BlackHole` and its conversion from `RuntimeError`.
- Added unit tests in `host_fns.rs` to verify `heap_force` behavior for all three thunk states.

All 69 unit tests in `tidepool-codegen` are passing, along with all tests in `tidepool-heap`.

Previously, these registries were cleaned up manually at the end of `run()` and `run_pure()`. However, early returns (e.g., via the `?` operator or explicit error paths) would bypass this cleanup, leaving stale state in the thread-local storage.

### Changes:
- Defined `RegistryGuard` with a `Drop` implementation that calls the `clear_*` functions.
- Updated `run()` and `run_pure()` to use `RegistryGuard` after installing the registries.
- Removed all manual cleanup blocks from `run()` and `run_pure()`.

Verified with `cargo check -p tidepool-codegen` and `cargo test -p tidepool-codegen`.

prs.title: feat: tail-call optimization for JIT-compiled lambdas
prs.body:
## Summary

- Tail-position `App` nodes in lambda bodies store callee+arg into VMContext fields and return null instead of `call_indirect`, enabling deep recursion (100K+ iterations) without stack overflow
- Non-tail callers detect null returns and resolve pending tail calls via `trampoline_resolve` host function
- Frame walker updated to skip non-JIT frames, supporting JIT→Host→JIT sandwiches (heap_force, trampoline_resolve)
- Resolution loops added to `effect_machine.rs` (step/call_closure) and `jit_machine.rs` (run_pure)

## Changes

| File | What |
|------|------|
| `emit/expr.rs` (+215) | `SetTailPosition` work item, `emit_tail_app` for tail Apps, null-check + trampoline call for non-tail Apps |
| `effect_machine.rs` (+26) | `resolve_tail_calls` helper called from `step()` and `call_closure()` |
| `jit_machine.rs` (+25) | Inline resolution loop in `run_pure()` with signal protection |
| `gc/frame_walker.rs` (+9/-13) | Skip non-JIT frames instead of breaking on first non-JIT frame |
| `tests/tco.rs` (new) | 3 integration tests: deep 100K recursion, moderate 1K, mixed tail/non-tail |

## Design

Builds on the VMContext infrastructure from ec050b6 (`tail_callee`/`tail_arg` fields, `trampoline_resolve`).

Key correctness arguments:
- No new GC safepoints in JIT tail-call path (null check is just a branch)
- VMContext tail fields don't need GC roots (set immediately before return, consumed immediately by resolver)
- `compile_expr` intentionally does NOT enable tail position — only lambda bodies get TCO, since raw JIT callers (test harnesses) may lack resolution loops

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test -p tidepool-codegen test_tco` — 3 TCO tests pass
- [x] `cargo test --workspace` — no regressions

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

prs.title: feat: add tests for error sentinel deferral
prs.body:
This PR adds comprehensive tests for the error sentinel deferral feature in `tidepool-codegen`.
GHC Core often hoists `error` calls into `let` bindings that are only intended to be forced on impossible branches. This feature ensures such bindings are bound to a poison closure instead of being evaluated eagerly by our JIT, preventing premature runtime errors.

Added tests cover:
- `LetNonRec` with error sentinel RHS (deferred correctly)
- `LetNonRec` forced (returns poison closure)
- `LetRec` with error sentinel in a binding (deferred correctly)
- `LetRec` with no recursive bindings (deferred correctly)
- Error sentinel detection in complex RHS (e.g., `Con(error_var)`)
- Verification that non-error bindings are not deferred.

Verified with `cargo test -p tidepool-codegen --test emit_expr`.

prs.title: feat: expand signal protection scope in jit_machine.rs
prs.body:
This PR expands the signal protection scope in `jit_machine.rs` to include calls to `heap_to_value` and `value_to_heap`. 

### Changes:
1. Added `Signal(crate::signal_safety::SignalError)` variant to `JitError` enum.

These changes ensure that any `SIGSEGV` or other fatal signals occurring during marshalling between the Rust heap and the JIT heap are caught and returned as a `JitError::Signal` instead of crashing the entire process.

prs.title: feat: use pthread_exit in signal handler fallback
prs.body:
This PR updates the signal handler fallback path in `tidepool-codegen/src/signal_safety.rs`. 

When a signal (SIGILL, SIGSEGV, etc.) occurs outside of the JIT context (i.e., `JMP_BUF` is null), we now use `pthread_exit` instead of re-raising the signal with `SIG_DFL`. This ensures that only the faulting thread (the detached eval thread) is terminated, rather than the entire process.

The MCP server already handles the case where the eval thread's channel sender drops as "Eval thread crashed", so this change allows the server to remain alive and report the error instead of being killed by the signal.

`pthread_exit` is POSIX async-signal-safe.

prs.title: Fix SIGSEGV in JIT by preventing heap_force from forcing TAG_CLOSURE
prs.body:
`test_jit_console_roundtrip` (and likely other complex JIT tests involving closures within effect handlers) was failing with a `SIGSEGV` during execution.

The root cause was that `heap_force` in `tidepool-codegen/src/host_fns.rs` was still attempting to force `TAG_CLOSURE` objects. It did this by reading the closure's `code_ptr` and invoking it with a `null` argument. 

Since the addition of lazy thunk codegen (`TAG_THUNK`), `TAG_CLOSURE` strictly represents lambda values, which are already in Weak Head Normal Form (WHNF). Furthermore, the JIT-compiled closure code expects its arguments, and passing `null` resulted in a segmentation fault when the closure attempted to use the missing argument.

This PR updates `heap_force` to immediately return the current object for all tags other than `TAG_THUNK`, correctly treating `TAG_CLOSURE`, `TAG_CON`, and `TAG_LIT` as WHNF.
